description.para: Definitions to be used with Endpoint related function that require a 'Direction' parameter.

NOTE: The direction is always defined from the perspective of the HOST! This means that an IN endpoint on the device is used for sending data while the OUT endpoint on the device is used for receiving data.

briefdescription.para: Endpoint direction IN.
Endpoint direction OUT.

description.para: Definitions to be used with speed.

briefdescription.para: High Speed.
Full Speed.

sectiondef.header: USB device specific global configuration constants.

briefdescription.para: Maximum End Points.
End point Zero.
Max device address.
Disconnected state.
Reset State.
Chirp J Test.
Chirp K Test.
Chirp SE0 Test.
Packet Test.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XUsb_EpHandlerFunc)(void *CallBackRef, u8 EpNum, u32 EventType)
memberdef.name: XUsb_EpHandlerFunc

briefdescription.para: This data type defines the callback function to be used for Endpoint handlers.

parameterdescription.para: is the Callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the handler, and is passed back to the upper layer when the handler is called.
is the endpoint that caused the event.
is the type of the event that occurred on that endpoint.

briefdescription.para: This data type defines the callback function to be used for the general interrupt handler.

parameterdescription.para: is the Callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the handler, and is passed back to the upper layer when the handler is called.
is the type of the event that caused the interrupt.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a XUsb instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para:
The initialization entails:
Initialize all members of the XUsb structure.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUsb instance of the USB device.
ConfigPtr
is a pointer to a XUsb_Config configuration structure. This structure will contain the requested configuration for the device. Typically, this is a local structure and the content of which will be copied into the configuration structure within XUsb.
EffectiveAddr
is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.
XST_SUCCESS no errors occurred.
XST_FAILURE an error occurred during initialization.
After calling XUsb_CfgInitialize() the USB device IS NOT READY for use. Before the USB device can be used its parameters must be configured. See xusb.h for details. Implemented in the file xusb.c

briefdescription.para: This function initializes USB End points.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

is pointer to a XUsb_Config configuration structure. This structure will contain the requested configuration for the device. Typically, this is a local structure and the content of which will be copied into the configuration structure within XUsb.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE an error occurred during initialization.

briefdescription.para: This function starts the USB Device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

briefdescription.para: This function stops the USB device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current frame number.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the USB device address.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the device address to be set.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the USB device into a given test mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the type of test to be performed.
is a pointer to the buffer containing the test packet.

simplesect.para: If the test mode is Test packet(TEST_PKT), then user needs to pass the address of the buffer containing the test packet. In other cases, the BufPtr parameter is not used and the user can send a NULL or any value. BufPtr parameter should be 32 bit aligned.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the DMA module of the USB device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

simplesect.para: After the DMA reset, only the DMA related logic part of the USB device will be reset and all the DMA related registers will be reset to the default values. Upon DMA Reset, any DMA transfer in progress will be stopped.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the DMA registers with the given values to initiate a DMA data transfer.

detaileddescription.para: This function is called by the XUsb_EpDataSend and XUsb_EpDataRecv functions.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the source address from where the data is to be read.
is the destination address to where the data is to be written.
is the amount of data that can be transferred. The maximum data transfer can be 1024.

simplesect.para: This function doesn't guarantee that the transfer is done successfully. This function only initiates the DMA transfer.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the USB error counter register and returns the error counters information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is a pointer to the 8 bit bitstuff error counter.
is a pointer to the 8 bit pid error counter.
is a pointer to the 8 bit crc error counter.

briefdescription.para: This function initiates the USB ULPI PHY register read transaction.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns the busy status if the earlier transaction is still in progress and returns the PHY register data upon successful read transaction.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the address of the PHY register.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_BUSY: The previous PHY transaction is still in progress.

simplesect.para: This function waits till the BUSY bit is cleared in the ULPI PHY resgiter and then reads the register. The user of this API should note that the PHY interrupt should be ignored during read operation.

briefdescription.para: This function initiates the USB ULPI PHY register write transaction.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns the busy status if the earlier transaction is still in progress and returns a success upon successful write transaction initiation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the address of the PHY register. counter.
is the data to be written to PHY register.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_BUSY: The previous PHY transaction is still in progress.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the Serial Interface Engine.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

simplesect.para: After the SIE reset, only the SIE state machine logic part of the USB device will be reset and starts from Init state.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified endpoint for all operations.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the endpoint number which has to be enabled for operations.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified endpoint for all operations.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the endpoint number which has to be disabled for operations.

briefdescription.para: This function configures a specific end point with the given configuration data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the endpoint number which has to be configured.
is a pointer to the endpoint configuration structure.

briefdescription.para: This function copies the transmit data to the end point buffer and enables the buffer for transmission.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the endpoint number.
is a pointer to buffer containing the data to be sent.
is the number of data bytes to be sent.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the send operation could not be performed due to non availability of the ping pong buffers in the DPRAM.

simplesect.para: Success doesn't imply that the data is actually transmitted, it only confirms that the DPRAM buffer is updated with send data and the core is enabled for transmitting the data.

briefdescription.para: This function copies the received data from end point buffer to the buffer passed and then makes the device ready for receiving data again into the same end point buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the endpoint number.
is a pointer to buffer where data is to be copied.
is the number of data bytes to be received.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if there is no received data in any of the ping pong buffers.

briefdescription.para: This function stalls operations for the specified endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the endpoint number which has to be stalled for operations.

simplesect.para: This function does not guaranty the Stall operation, it only sets the Stall bit in the Endpoint configuration register.

briefdescription.para: This function unstalls operations for the specified endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the endpoint number for which the unstall operations are to be carried out.

simplesect.para: This function does not guaranty the Stall operation, it only sets the Stall bit in the Endpoint configuration register.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the number of isochronous transfers in a microframe for a given endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the endpoint number.
is the number of transfers in a microframe.

simplesect.para: The values allowed for number of transfers is 1, 2 and 3. This function should be called after all the endpoints are configured. The allowed value for EpNum is 1 to 7

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XUSB_STATUS_*_MASK bits defined in xusb_l.h.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be disabled. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XUSB_STATUS_*_MASK bits defined in xusb_l.h.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the USB driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function is the first-level interrupt handler for the USB core. All USB interrupts will be handled here. Depending on the type of the interrupt, second level interrupt handler may be called. Second level interrupt handlers will be registered by the user using the XUsb_IntrSetHandler() and/or XUsb_EpSetHandler() functions.
This handler reads the interrupt status from the Interrupt Status Register, determines the source of the interrupts, calls the corresponding callbacks and finally clears the interrupts.
The interrupt from the USB driver has to be connected to the interrupt controller and the handler has to be registered with the interrupt system.
Applications using this driver are responsible for providing the callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XUsb_SetHandler() during the initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance that just interrupted.

simplesect.para: No user handler is defined for the DMA interrupts. The DMA interrupt status is updated in the DMA status variables defined under the USB driver instance.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the general interrupt (interrupts other than the endpoint and error interrupts).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler. The user can disable a handler by setting the callback function pointer to NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler for endpoint events.

parameterdescription.para: is a Pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the Number of the endpoint.
is the address of the callback function. It can be NULL if the user wants to disable the handler entry.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked. This can be NULL.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler. The user can disable a handler by setting the callback function pointer to NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the error events.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler. The user can disable a handler by setting the callback function pointer to NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the DMA events.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler. The user can disable a handler by setting the callback function pointer to NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the ULPI PHY events.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler. The user can disable a handler by setting the callback function pointer to NULL.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

listitem.para: A pointer to the configuration found.
NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found.

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.
